title: [VMIC-A5] Status bar routing indicator
scope: Render TTS routing state in the compact and expanded status/metrics strip

test_cases.status_bar_virtual_mic_indicator: pass - compact strip includes vmic route and truncated virtual device name
test_cases.status_bar_missing_virtual_mic_warning: pass - compact strip surfaces missing virtual mic warning
test_cases.layout_80x24: pass - PTY layout remains readable at 80x24
test_cases.layout_120x40: pass - PTY layout remains readable at 120x40
test_cases.layout_200x50: pass - PTY layout remains readable at 200x50

commands.name: focused-status-tests
commands.command: cargo +stable-x86_64-pc-windows-gnu test --bin tui-translator -- status_bar_speakers_indicator && cargo +stable-x86_64-pc-windows-gnu test --bin tui-translator -- status_bar_virtual_mic_indicator && cargo +stable-x86_64-pc-windows-gnu test --bin tui-translator -- status_bar_missing_virtual_mic_warning

commands.name: snapshot-tests
commands.summary: 324 passed; snapshots updated for route indicators

commands.name: pty-layout-tests
commands.command: cargo +stable-x86_64-pc-windows-gnu test --test pty -- layout_80x24 layout_120x40 layout_200x50

notes: TtsRouteStatus is derived from AppConfig at draw time, so config reloads are reflected without adding persistent AppState fields.
Compact status uses short labels (spk, vmic, both) and truncates virtual device names to keep the strip readable.
Expanded status shows a fuller route label while preserving existing metrics rows.
The existing T shortcut behavior and help text are unchanged.
